c.d: The number of active users on the platform, indicating its popularity and reach.
c.s: App Store, Google Play Store, Company Reports

c.d: The maximum number of contacts to whom a message can be broadcast simultaneously.
The operating systems and devices on which the app is available (e.g., iOS, Android, Web).
c.s: App Store, Google Play Store, Official Website

c.d: The ability to customize messages with placeholders for recipient-specific information.
The app's ability to integrate with other services or platforms, such as CRM systems or email marketing tools.
The pricing structure of the app, including free tiers, subscription plans, or pay-as-you-go options.
c.s: Official Pricing Pages, App Store Listings

c.d: The presence of security measures like end-to-end encryption to protect user data and communications.
The ability to schedule messages to be sent at a later time or date.
The capability to send various types of media, such as images, videos, and documents, in addition to text messages.
Feedback from users regarding their experience with the app, indicating overall satisfaction and reliability.
c.s: App Store, Google Play Store, Review Aggregator Sites
